q9_gambling_types_slot_machines_at_racet.question_label: Gambling Type: Slot Machines at Racetracks

q10_offshore_awareness.question_label: Awareness of Offshore Online Casino Gambling

q17j.question_label: Belief: Tribal Gaming Keeps Revenue in Louisiana

q17k.question_label: Belief: Apps Designed to Be Addictive

q18a_corporate_unfair.question_label: Argument Matchup: Corporate Fairness vs. Tribal Revenue

metrics.label: Allowing only Louisiana's tribes to operate online casino gaming is unfair. It gives one group of operators a special deal and shuts other companies out of the market.
The companies calling this "unfair" are big corporate gaming operators based outside of Louisiana. Letting Louisiana tribes operate online casino gaming means that more money stays in Louisiana.

q18b_addiction_protection.question_label: Argument Matchup: Addiction Risk vs. Consumer Safeguards

metrics.label: Online casino gambling apps are designed to be addictive, and would put a casino in every Louisiana smartphone, making it easier for kids and addicted Louisianans to lose money their families can't afford. Governor Landry would be putting tax revenue ahead of Louisiana families.

Illegal offshore gambling sites already operate in Louisiana today with no rules. A regulated tribal-state framework would add age verification, consumer protections, enforcement, and safeguards that do not exist today.

q18c_freedom_protection.question_label: Argument Matchup: Adult Freedom vs. State Protection

metrics.label: Adults in Louisiana should be free to make their own choices about legal entertainment, including online casino gaming, just like they are with alcohol.
The state has a responsibility to protect Louisiana families from gambling industries that profit from addiction.

q18d_voter_say.question_label: Argument Matchup: Voter Say vs. Regulated Tribal Framework

metrics.label: Opponents say this would let the Governor approve a major gambling expansion without voters having a direct say, and could open the door to online gambling in communities that have voted against gambling.
Supporters say this is not a gambling free-for-all. It is a limited, regulated tribal framework with strict guardrails aimed at replacing illegal offshore activity.
None. I oppose this no matter how the revenue is used.
Very firm. Nothing would change my mind.
Fairly firm but could imagine my view shifting with strong arguments.
Open to persuasion in either direction.
I really don't have a strong view either way.
